section.heading: Reserves: separation for absence without authority or sentence to imprisonment
section.chapeau: The President or the Secretary concerned may drop from the rolls of the armed force concerned any Reserve—

paragraph.content: who has been absent without authority for at least three months;
who may be separated under section 12687 of this title by reason of a sentence to confinement adjudged by a court-martial; or

who is sentenced to confinement in a Federal or State penitentiary or correctional institution after having been found guilty of an offense by a court other than a court-martial or other military court, and whose sentence has become final.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title XVI, § 1662(i)(1), Oct. 5, 1994, 108 Stat. 2998; amended Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title V, § 563(b)(2), Feb. 10, 1996, 110 Stat. 325.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: Provisions similar to those in this section were contained in section 1163(b) of this title, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 103–337, § 1662(i)(2).

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1996—Pub. L. 104–106 struck out “or” at end of par. (1), added par. (2), and redesignated former par. (2) as (3).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Dec. 1, 1994, except as otherwise provided, see section 1691 of Pub. L. 103–337, set out as a note under section 10001 of this title.
